content.content:
# A'Space OS Landing Page Requirements

## Vision
A multi-tenant landing page for A'Space OS, inspired by the 11th Doctor's brainstorming. 
Style: "Dark Mode" Solarpunk, minimalist, and biomimetic.

## Experience 1: Desktop (The Cockpit)
- **Hero:** "Your Life, Orchestrated. Bigger on the inside."
- **CTAs:** [ S'incarner (Sign Up) ], [ Reprendre les commandes (Log In) ].
- **Visual:** Use a representation of the Ikigai / Life Wheel Metrics (The 2026 command terminal).
- **Promise Section:** Solarpunk grid showcasing 8 Hubs (LD01-LD08).
- **Arsenal Section:** Focus on acceleration methods: 12WY, PARA, GTD, and SOB.

## Experience 2: Mobile (The Curiosity Filter)
- **Architecture:** Microsite vitrine for email capture and ideological triage.
- **Hero:** "Your keys to the A'Space Core are ready. The universe is bigger on the inside."
- **Visual:** A mysterious, blurred preview of the Life Wheel with overlay: "Desktop Console Access Required for Orchestration."
- **Manifesto:** Textual section explaining the Desktop-First philosophy to protect the Architect's energy and ensure autonomy.
- **Action:** [ Claim Your Keys (Sign Up) ] with email field.
- **Redirection:** Invite to return to "main terminal" for full onboarding.

attachments.content: Create a high-fidelity, polished design. Follow the instructions about design in your system prompt, particularly the 'How to do design work' section. Use the design_canvas starter component, or make a full-bleed prototype and offer options via Tweaks.
attachments.name: Hi-fi design

attachments.content: Create a fully interactive prototype with realistic state management and transitions. Use React useState/useEffect for dynamic behavior. Include hover states, click interactions, form validation, animated transitions, and multi-step navigation flows. It should feel like a real working app, not a static mockup.
attachments.name: Interactive prototype

attachments.content:
[Design System] The user selected the **Design System** design system. This is a binding choice — every visual must follow it, even if the task seems irrelevant. Don't invent colors, type, spacing, or components not grounded here.

    Explore it to find what you need:
    - Always copy out the fonts and colors you need
    - For prototypes and designs, always copy out any relevant components
    - If the design system contains existing mocks of products, and you were asked to design or prototype something similar, copy and fork those mocks to start your design. This helps you make high-quality designs.
    
    Explore it quickly to find relevant UI kits (e.g. mocks of existing products you can copy and fork)

Full system at `/projects/d34bd205-a41f-42cf-a693-3960c69ba561/`. Before producing any visuals, explore it: call `list_files("/projects/d34bd205-a41f-42cf-a693-3960c69ba561/")` to see the structure, then `read_file` the README/base.md or whatever index file it has. Don't guess at the design system's contents.

For assets and UI kits beyond the guide: `read_file("/projects/d34bd205-a41f-42cf-a693-3960c69ba561/<path>")` and `copy_files` to bring them into the current project.
attachments.name: Design System (design system)

questions.title: How should the Ikigai / Life Wheel look on the hero?

options: Lean into it — bilingual, mystical, Whovian Easter eggs front and center
English primary, French as accent (just the CTAs + key phrases)
French primary, English as accent
Restrained — cryptic but mostly plain, the weirdness earns itself
Other
questions.title: Copywriting voice — the brief mixes French + English + Doctor Who references. How should it read?

options: I'll paste them in a follow-up
Invent plausible names in the solarpunk/life-design vocabulary
Just use codes (LD01, LD02…) with cryptic one-liners
Other
questions.subtitle: If not I'll invent plausible ones (Life Design domains: Mind, Body, Craft, Relations, Wealth, etc.)
questions.title: The 8 Hubs (LD01–LD08) — do you have names for them?

options: One-line poetic tagline per method (assume audience knows these)
Tagline + 2-3 line explainer per method
Full card with icon, name, what-it-does, and a small diagram
Decide for me
questions.title: The Arsenal section (12WY, PARA, GTD, SOB) — how much explanation?

options: Heavy: locked vault aesthetic, real friction, almost adversarial
Medium: clear that mobile is limited but respectful of the visitor
Light: looks like a normal teaser with a gentle 'come back on desktop' note
Decide for me
questions.subtitle: The brief calls it a 'Curiosity Filter' and 'ideological triage' — intentional friction.
questions.title: Mobile experience — how 'gatekept' should it feel?

options: Restrained — subtle pulses on the Life Wheel, hover states, that's it
Medium — animated terminal typing, wheel rotates on scroll, metric tickers
Ambitious — living biomimetic backgrounds, particle systems, the wheel breathes
Decide for me
questions.title: Motion & interactivity ambition?

options: Yes — give me 2–3 togglable variants to compare
Just one strong direction, no variants
Decide for me
questions.title: Should I explore variations via Tweaks (color variant, wheel style, copy tone)?

options: One page that responds (desktop view + mobile view in same file)
Two separate files (desktop.html + mobile.html) linked together
Design canvas showing both side-by-side at their native sizes
Decide for me
questions.title: Single page with both experiences, or two files?

options: Yes — I'll attach in a follow-up
No, design a wordmark/logomark as part of this
No, use typographic treatment only (no mark)

toolCall.output: Questions form is now displayed to the user. Inform them you've asked some questions (which should be visible on their screen in the preview pane) and end your turn. Wait for their response. If they don't respond within 5 minutes, the form auto-submits with defaults.

content.content:
Questions answered:
- mood: 3
- life_wheel_viz: 1
- tone: Lean into it — bilingual, mystical, Whovian Easter eggs front and center
- hub_identity: I'll paste them in a follow-up
- arsenal_depth: Full card with icon, name, what-it-does, and a small diagram
- mobile_attitude: Heavy: locked vault aesthetic, real friction, almost adversarial
- motion: Ambitious — living biomimetic backgrounds, particle systems, the wheel breathes
- variations: Yes — give me 2–3 togglable variants to compare
- deliverable_shape: One page that responds (desktop view + mobile view in same file)
- brand_assets: No, use typographic treatment only (no mark)

I've got a strong direction. Let me lock in the system and build.

**Direction:**
- **Palette:** Bio Teal on near-black (option 3) — `#081012` base, `#4fd1c5` primary, `#a0f0e8` highlight, with organic accents
- **Life Wheel:** Layered concentric rings + radial spokes (option 2 — the orbital/radar style)
- **Voice:** Bilingual mystical — "S'incarner", "Bigger on the inside", Whovian easter eggs
- **Motion:** Ambitious — breathing wheel, particle drift, scroll-reactive backgrounds
- **Layout:** Responsive single page (desktop cockpit ↔ mobile vault)
- **Tweaks:** palette variant, wheel style, intensity

I'll use placeholder hub names (LD01–LD08) with evocative one-liners until you paste yours.Let me get the tweaks panel starter ready, then build.Now the main HTML scaffold with tokens, fonts, and the React mount:Now the Life Wheel — the centerpiece. Breathing radial cockpit with live-looking metrics.Now the desktop experience — hero cockpit and section layouts.I need to check the tweaks-panel helpers I'm using exist with those names.I spotted two issues: `TweakSection` takes `label` not `title`, `TweakRadio` options are string arrays or `{value,label}` — need to verify, and `TweakRadio` takes no `label`. Also the `useTweaks` destructure from `window` in `app.jsx` happens at module load — those may not be assigned yet since React/Babel script order. Let me also check that destructuring.I need to fix the TweaksPanel API usage. `TweakSection` needs `label`, and I need to pass `label` to `TweakRadio`. Also `useTweaks` is called outside the component so top-level destructuring from `window` is fine because `app.jsx` loads last. Let me fix the props and also fix a bug in mobile.jsx — it uses `Corners` which is defined in `hubs.jsx` but exported. Let me verify and fix.**A'Space OS landing is live.**

**Experience 1 — Cockpit (desktop):**
- Hero: "Your Life, Orchestrated. *Bigger on the inside.*" with animated terminal boot, dual CTAs (S'incarner / Reprendre les commandes)
- Breathing Life Wheel with 8 sectors, rotating reticles, live cockpit readouts (coherence, alignment, cadence, 432 Hz harmonic)
- Solarpunk 8-hub grid (LD01 Foyer → LD08 Horizon) — hover reveals descriptions
- Arsenal: full cards for 12WY, PARA, GTD, SOB, each with a hand-drawn schematic diagram

**Experience 2 — Vestibule (mobile, <820px):**
- Adversarial lock: blurred wheel + amber lock glyph + scrambling "DESKTOP CONSOLE ACCESS REQUIRED" readout
- Status console showing "clearance: vestibule only · core: sealed"
- Manifesto on Desktop-First philosophy
- Email capture ("Leave a signal. One message. No others. Ever.")
- Redirect button back to main terminal

**Living background:** drifting bioluminescent motes + three slow auroral wave bands on canvas; aurora color follows palette.

**Tweaks (toolbar toggle):**
- Palette — Bio Teal / Moss / Amber
- View — Auto / Cockpit / Vault (to preview mobile on desktop)
- Intensity slider + Breathing toggle

**Next steps when ready:**
- Paste the real LD01–LD08 hub names and I'll swap the placeholders
- Real copy for the Arsenal explainers if you have them
- Logo/wordmark if you decide to add one later

toolCall.name: grep

contentBlocks.text: I spotted two issues: `TweakSection` takes `label` not `title`, `TweakRadio` options are string arrays or `{value,label}` — need to verify, and `TweakRadio` takes no `label`. Also the `useTweaks` destructure from `window` in `app.jsx` happens at module load — those may not be assigned yet since React/Babel script order. Let me also check that destructuring.

toolCall.name: read_file

contentBlocks.text: I need to fix the TweaksPanel API usage. `TweakSection` needs `label`, and I need to pass `label` to `TweakRadio`. Also `useTweaks` is called outside the component so top-level destructuring from `window` is fine because `app.jsx` loads last. Let me fix the props and also fix a bug in mobile.jsx — it uses `Corners` which is defined in `hubs.jsx` but exported. Let me verify and fix.

input.new_string:
// App root — responsive switcher + tweaks panel.

const DEFAULTS

<TweaksPanel title="Tweaks">
        <TweakSection label="Palette" />
        <TweakRadio
          label="Mood"
          value={tweaks.palette}
          onChange={(v) => setTweak("palette", v)}
          options={[
            { value: "bio",   label: "Bio Teal" },
            { value: "moss",  label: "Moss" },
            { value: "amber", label: "Amber" },
          ]}
        />

        <TweakSection label="View" />
        <TweakRadio
          label="Mode"
          value={tweaks.forceView}
          onChange={(v) => setTweak("forceView", v)}
          options={[
            { value: "auto",    label: "Auto" },
            { value: "desktop", label: "Cockpit" },
            { value: "mobile",  label: "Vault" },
          ]}
        />

        <TweakSection label="Atmosphere" />
        <TweakSlider
          label="Intensity"
          value={tweaks.intensity}
          onChange={(v) => setTweak("intensity", v)}
          min={0} max={1.5} step={0.05}
        />
        <TweakToggle
          label="Breathing"
          value={tweaks.breathing}
          onChange={(v) => setTweak("breathing", v)}
        />
      </TweaksPanel>
input.old_string:
<TweaksPanel title="Tweaks">
        <TweakSection title="Palette">
          <TweakRadio
            value={tweaks.palette}
            onChange={(v) => setTweak("palette", v)}

              { value: "bio",   label: "Bio Teal" },
              { value: "moss",  label: "Moss + Gold" },
              { value: "amber", label: "Amber Dusk" },
            ]}
          />
        </TweakSection>

        <TweakSection title="View">
          <TweakRadio
            value={tweaks.forceView}
            onChange={(v) => setTweak("forceView", v)}
            options={[
              { value: "auto",    label: "Auto (responsive)" },
              { value: "desktop", label: "Cockpit (desktop)" },
              { value: "mobile",  label: "Vestibule (mobile)" },
            ]}
          />
        </TweakSection>

        <TweakSection title="Atmosphere">
          <TweakSlider
            label="Intensity"
            value={tweaks.intensity}
            onChange={(v) => setTweak("intensity", v)}
            min={0} max={1.5} step={0.05}
          />
          <TweakToggle
            label="Breathing"
            value={tweaks.breathing}
            onChange={(v) => setTweak("breathing", v)}
          />
        </TweakSection>
      </TweaksPanel>
